findings.description: The retry loop break condition was changed from `if (lastOutput.action !== 'failed') break;` to `if (lastOutput.processed || lastOutput.action === 'blocked') break;`. This was NOT in the plan. Plan Step 2 explicitly says: 'No structural change needed here beyond the rename.' The old condition broke on ANY non-failed action (including `action: 'fixed'` when verification failed), leaving the comment in `replied` state for cross-poll handling. The new condition causes the loop to retry verification failures within the same poll, exhausting ESCALATION_BUDGET and blocking immediately — contradicting Design Decision 5 which states verification failures stay in `replied` state and are handled by `verifyOrphaned` across polls.
findings.fix: Revert to the original break condition: `if (lastOutput.action !== 'failed') break;`

findings.description: The `makeDeps` helper uses `IncrementingShaGitPort` which doesn't set up `ancestorResults` or `logBetweenResults`. When `verifyComment` runs in the runner, it calls `git.remoteRef` (→ undefined → fixCommitOnRemote = false), `git.isAncestor` (→ false), `git.logBetween` (→ []). This causes verification to fail for happy-path tests that should pass. Plan Step 4 says to add these mock setups.
findings.fix: Update `makeDeps` or individual tests to set up `ancestorResults` and `logBetweenResults` on the git port, so that `verifyComment`'s ancestry/log checks pass for happy-path tests.

findings.description: Happy-path test expects `summary.outcome === 'ALL_RESOLVED'` but gets `'PARTIAL_PROGRESS'`. Root cause: verifyComment fails because git mocks aren't set up (ancestorResults/logBetweenResults missing).
findings.fix: Set up git mock data for ancestor/log checks, or revert break condition so verification failures don't block.

findings.description: Test expects comment state to be `'replied'` after poll but gets `'blocked'`. Root cause: break condition change causes the retry loop to exhaust ESCALATION_BUDGET within the same poll instead of leaving comment in `replied` state for cross-poll handling.
Test expects 2 tasks processed but gets 0. Root cause: comments are blocked by the retry loop exhausting ESCALATION_BUDGET before processing completes.
Test expects comment state to be `'replied'` after poll but gets `'blocked'`. Root cause: break condition change.
Test expects comment state to be `'replied'` after poll but gets `'blocked'`. Root cause: break condition change.
Test expects `processedCount === 1` but gets 0. Root cause: comment is blocked by retry loop exhausting ESCALATION_BUDGET before processing.
Test expects comment state to be `'replied'` after poll but gets `'blocked'`. Root cause: break condition change.
Test expects `summary.outcome === 'ALL_RESOLVED'` but gets `'PARTIAL_PROGRESS'`. Root cause: verifyComment fails due to missing git mock setup.
findings.fix: Set up git mock data for ancestor/log checks.

findings.description: Test expects `verifyCalls[1]!.startCommitSha` to differ from `verifyCalls[0]!.startCommitSha` but they're both `'sha-2'`. Root cause: break condition change causes the first comment's retry loop to consume multiple attempts with the same runningStartSha, then blocks before the second comment gets its own verifyCall.

Test expects `processedCount === 2` but gets 1. Root cause: break condition change causes the first comment to be blocked after exhausting ESCALATION_BUDGET, so only the second comment gets processed.
